properties.name: Standard_Flow_Universal_Commissioning_Availability
properties.claim: A Standard Commissioning Flow device SHALL be available for initial commissioning by any Matter commissioner
properties.violation: If a Standard Flow device restricts which commissioners can perform initial commissioning, it creates vendor lock-in and violates the interoperability guarantee. This could force users to use specific manufacturer commissioners.
properties.attack_vector: Manufacturer creates Standard Flow device that secretly validates commissioner identity and rejects non-preferred commissioners, forcing users into ecosystem lock-in and preventing fair competition.

properties.name: Standard_Flow_Automatic_Advertisement_On_Power
properties.claim: A Standard Commissioning Flow device, when in factory-new state, SHALL start advertising automatically upon power on
properties.violation: If device fails to advertise automatically when factory-new and powered on, commissioning cannot begin. User cannot discover the device, leading to failed onboarding and potential device bricking.
properties.attack_vector: Attacker triggers a partial factory reset that leaves device in factory-new state but disables auto-advertising, making device undiscoverable and effectively bricked.

properties.name: Standard_Flow_Custom_Bits_Consistency
properties.claim: A Standard Commissioning Flow device SHALL set Custom Flow bits in the Onboarding Payload to indicate '0 - Standard Flow'
properties.violation: If Custom Flow bits mismatch the actual flow type, commissioner follows wrong commissioning procedure, leading to failed commissioning, incorrect user instructions, or bypassed security checks.
properties.attack_vector: Attacker modifies onboarding payload to set Custom Flow bits to '2' (Custom) on Standard Flow device, forcing commissioner to look up and follow manufacturer URL, enabling phishing or man-in-the-middle attacks.

properties.name: Standard_Flow_Pairing_Code_Inclusion
properties.claim: A Standard Commissioning Flow device SHALL follow the rules for Manual Pairing Code and QR Code Inclusion
properties.violation: Without proper pairing codes, secure proof-of-possession fails. Attacker can commission device without physical access, or user cannot commission device at all.
properties.attack_vector: Device ships without valid pairing code. Attacker with network proximity commissions device before legitimate owner, gaining persistent control.

properties.name: Standard_Flow_DCL_CustomFlow_Consistency
properties.claim: The DCL entries for Standard Commissioning Flow devices SHALL include the CommissioningCustomFlow field set to '0 - Standard'
properties.violation: Mismatch between device flow type and DCL metadata causes commissioner to execute wrong flow, misguiding user, failing commissioning, or following malicious URLs.
properties.attack_vector: Attacker compromises DCL entry for Standard Flow device, changing CommissioningCustomFlow to '2' and adding malicious CommissioningCustomFlowUrl. Commissioner redirects user to attacker-controlled website for credential harvesting.

properties.name: Standard_Flow_InitialSteps_PowerCycle_Required
properties.claim: The CommissioningModeInitialStepsHint field for Standard Flow SHALL be set to non-zero with bit 0 (Power Cycle) set to 1
properties.violation: If Power Cycle bit not set, commissioner cannot guide user to restart device to re-enter commissioning mode after advertisement period expires, leaving device unrecoverable without manufacturer documentation.
properties.attack_vector: Attacker with temporary network access blocks commissioning during initial advertisement window. Without Power Cycle guidance, user cannot restart commissioning, effectively DoS against device.

properties.name: Standard_Flow_InitialSteps_Instruction_Dependency
properties.claim: CommissioningModeInitialStepsInstruction SHALL be set when CommissioningModeInitialStepsHint has mandatory Pairing Instruction dependency
properties.violation: Without mandatory instructions, commissioner cannot guide user through required steps, commissioning fails, device becomes unusable.
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits missing instructions by social engineering user with false instructions that compromise device security during commissioning.

properties.name: UserIntent_Flow_Universal_Commissioning_Availability
properties.claim: A User-Intent Commissioning Flow device SHALL be available for initial commissioning by any Matter commissioner
properties.violation: If User-Intent Flow device restricts commissioner access, it violates interoperability and creates vendor lock-in, preventing user choice of commissioner.
properties.attack_vector: Manufacturer implements hidden commissioner whitelist in User-Intent device, rejecting commissioning attempts from competitor commissioners, forcing users into specific ecosystem.

properties.name: UserIntent_Flow_No_Auto_Advertisement
properties.claim: A User-Intent Commissioning Flow device, when factory-new, SHALL NOT start advertising automatically upon power on
properties.violation: If User-Intent device auto-advertises without user intent signal, attacker can commission device immediately upon power-on without user awareness, before user has chance to secure environment.
properties.attack_vector: User powers on User-Intent device (e.g., security camera) in unsecured location. Attacker with network proximity immediately commissions device before user provides intent signal, gaining access to sensitive device.

properties.name: UserIntent_Flow_User_Interaction_Required
properties.claim: To place User-Intent Flow device into advertising mode, user interaction beyond power application is required
properties.formal: State(device) = FactoryNew ∧ DeviceType(device) = UserIntentFlow → EnterAdvertising(device) requires UserInteractionBeyondPower(device)
properties.violation: Without explicit user intent signal, device is vulnerable to drive-by commissioning attacks during initial power-on window.
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits automatic advertising by positioning themselves near device during initial unboxing/power-on, commissioning device before legitimate owner intends to commission it.

properties.name: UserIntent_Flow_Device_Manufacturer_Artifact_Exclusion
properties.claim: If User-Intent Flow device requires Device Manufacturer setup artifact beyond documentation for user interaction, it is Custom Flow not User-Intent Flow
properties.violation: Misclassifying Custom Flow as User-Intent Flow misleads commissioners about commissioning requirements, causing failed commissioning when commissioner lacks manufacturer app.
properties.attack_vector: Device claims User-Intent Flow but secretly requires manufacturer app for commissioning. Commissioner attempts standard flow, fails, user cannot commission device without discovering hidden app requirement.

properties.name: UserIntent_Flow_Pairing_Code_Inclusion
properties.claim: A User-Intent Commissioning Flow device SHALL follow the rules for Manual Pairing Code and QR Code Inclusion
properties.violation: Without valid pairing codes, secure proof-of-possession fails. Device can be commissioned without physical access, or legitimate user cannot commission.
properties.attack_vector: User-Intent device lacks proper pairing code. Attacker waits for user to trigger intent signal (e.g., button press), then races to commission device before user completes commissioning.

properties.name: UserIntent_Flow_DCL_CustomFlow_Consistency
properties.claim: DCL entries for User-Intent Flow devices SHALL include CommissioningCustomFlow field set to '1 - User Intent'
properties.violation: Mismatch between device flow type and DCL causes commissioner to use wrong flow, misguiding user with incorrect instructions or following wrong URLs.
properties.attack_vector: Attacker modifies DCL to mark User-Intent device as Standard Flow. Commissioner incorrectly expects auto-advertising, user receives wrong guidance, commissioning fails or proceeds insecurely.

properties.name: UserIntent_Flow_PowerCycle_Bit_Zero
properties.claim: Bit 0 (Power Cycle) in CommissioningModeInitialStepsHint for User-Intent Flow SHALL be set to 0
properties.violation: If Power Cycle bit incorrectly set to 1, commissioner instructs user to power cycle to enter commissioning mode, but User-Intent device requires explicit user interaction, not power cycle. Instructions mislead user, commissioning fails.
properties.attack_vector: Corrupted DCL entry sets Power Cycle bit for User-Intent device. User follows incorrect guidance, repeatedly power cycles device without success, eventually abandons commissioning or seeks malicious third-party help.

properties.name: UserIntent_Flow_InitialSteps_Instruction_Dependency
properties.claim: CommissioningModeInitialStepsInstruction SHALL be set when CommissioningModeInitialStepsHint has mandatory Pairing Instruction dependency for User-Intent Flow
properties.violation: Missing mandatory instructions prevent commissioner from guiding user, commissioning fails, user cannot access device functionality.
properties.attack_vector: Attacker exploits instruction gap to provide fraudulent commissioning guidance via social engineering, directing user to compromise device security.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_Manufacturer_Interaction_Required
properties.claim: Custom Commissioning Flow device SHALL require interaction with custom steps guided by manufacturer service before commissioning by any commissioner
properties.formal: DeviceType(device) = CustomFlow → RequiresManufacturerGuidedSteps(device) before ∀ commissioner ∈ MatterCommissioners: canCommission(device, commissioner)
properties.violation: If Custom Flow device allows commissioning without manufacturer interaction, it violates flow definition and may skip security steps manufacturer intended to enforce.
properties.attack_vector: Attacker bypasses manufacturer-guided custom steps (e.g., account creation, ToS acceptance, security configuration) by directly commissioning device, evading audit trails and security controls.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_ESF_Payload_Inclusion
properties.claim: Custom Flow device supporting Enhanced Setup Flow SHALL include usable Onboarding Payload on-device or in packaging
properties.violation: Without onboarding payload, ESF-capable commissioner cannot commission device directly, forcing all users through custom flow even when ESF would be sufficient and more secure.
properties.attack_vector: Device lacks payload despite ESF support. All users must use manufacturer URL, creating single point of failure and phishing opportunity. Attacker sets up fake manufacturer site to harvest credentials.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_ESF_Pairing_Code_Rules
properties.claim: Custom Flow device with ESF support SHALL follow Manual Pairing Code and QR Code Inclusion rules
properties.violation: Invalid or missing pairing codes compromise proof-of-possession, enabling remote commissioning without physical access or preventing legitimate commissioning.
properties.attack_vector: Custom Flow device with broken pairing code allows attacker to commission without physical access after manufacturer custom flow makes device commissionable, bypassing physical security.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_Non_ESF_Payload_Optional
properties.claim: Custom Flow device not supporting ESF MAY include Onboarding Payload; if omitted, SHALL be provided through manufacturer means
properties.violation: If non-ESF Custom Flow device omits payload and fails to provide it via manufacturer means, user cannot obtain commissioning credentials, device cannot be commissioned.
properties.attack_vector: Attacker intercepts or blocks manufacturer means of providing payload, preventing legitimate user from obtaining commissioning credentials while attacker uses leaked or default credentials.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_CustomBits_Consistency
properties.claim: Custom Commissioning Flow device SHALL set Custom Flow bits in Onboarding Payload to indicate '2 - Custom'
properties.violation: Mismatch between actual flow and Custom Flow bits causes commissioner to follow wrong procedure, skipping required manufacturer steps or attempting standard commissioning when impossible.
properties.attack_vector: Attacker modifies payload to set Custom bits to '0' (Standard). Commissioner attempts direct commissioning, failing repeatedly while attacker uses correct custom flow to commission device first.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_DCL_CustomFlow_Field
properties.claim: DCL entries for Custom Flow devices SHALL include CommissioningCustomFlow field set to '2 - Custom'
properties.violation: DCL mismatch causes commissioner to use wrong flow, failing commissioning or skipping required security steps.
properties.attack_vector: Attacker corrupts DCL entry to mark Custom Flow device as Standard Flow. Commissioner bypasses manufacturer's mandatory security setup (e.g., account binding, ToS), commissioning device in insecure state.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_DCL_URL_Required
properties.claim: DCL entries for Custom Flow devices SHALL populate CommissioningCustomFlowUrl to indicate initial commissioning requires visiting this URL
properties.violation: Missing or insecure URL prevents commissioner from guiding user to required manufacturer setup, commissioning fails or proceeds via insecure channel.
properties.attack_vector: DCL entry has HTTP (not HTTPS) CommissioningCustomFlowUrl. Attacker performs MitM on HTTP connection, serving phishing page that harvests user credentials and device information.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_Commissioner_URL_Usage
properties.claim: When commissioner encounters Custom Flow device, SHOULD use CommissioningCustomFlowUrl to guide user unless alternative means available
properties.violation: If commissioner ignores CommissioningCustomFlowUrl without alternative means, user doesn't complete required manufacturer setup, commissioning fails or completes in insecure state.
properties.attack_vector: Commissioner bug causes it to skip Custom Flow URL. Device is commissioned without manufacturer's security setup. Attacker exploits missing security configuration to compromise device.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_URL_Expansion_Required
properties.claim: If commissioner follows CommissioningCustomFlowUrl after user request, SHALL expand it as described in URL format specification
properties.violation: Incorrect URL expansion leads to malformed requests, failing manufacturer flow, or leaking sensitive data in URL parameters.
properties.attack_vector: Commissioner fails to URL-encode onboarding payload in MTop parameter. Payload contains special characters that break URL. Manufacturer server misparses request, logs sensitive plaintext data, or returns error exposing internal state.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_ESF_Priority
properties.claim: If EnhancedSetupFlowOptions indicates ESF support and commissioner supports required ESF flags, SHOULD perform ESF instead of following CommissioningCustomFlowUrl
properties.violation: Ignoring ESF when supported forces unnecessary custom flow, exposing user to phishing risk and creating single point of failure at manufacturer URL.
properties.attack_vector: Commissioner ignores ESF support, always redirecting to manufacturer URL. Attacker registers lookalike domain, corrupts DCL to insert fake URL, phishes users during commissioning.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_Internet_Dependency
properties.claim: Custom Flow typically requires internet access to access URL, initial commissioning may fail without internet connection
properties.violation: Custom Flow creates hard dependency on internet availability. Attacker blocking internet access can DoS commissioning. User in offline environment cannot commission device.
properties.attack_vector: Attacker performs selective internet blocking during commissioning, preventing access to manufacturer URL while maintaining local network access. User cannot commission, creating DoS condition.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_Platform_Dependency
properties.claim: If Custom Flow requires manufacturer app, it must be available for popular platforms, some commissioner platforms may be unable to perform initial commissioning
properties.violation: Platform-specific app requirement creates commissioning lock-out for users on unsupported platforms, enabling manufacturer lock-in and excluding certain commissioner types.
properties.attack_vector: Manufacturer only provides iOS app for Custom Flow setup. Users on Android, Windows, Linux, or headless commissioners cannot commission device, forcing platform choice or device abandonment.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_URL_Query_Format
properties.claim: CommissioningCustomFlowUrl query component SHALL use '&' delimiter and 'name=<value>' format with proper URL encoding
properties.violation: Malformed query string breaks URL parsing, causing manufacturer server to reject request or misinterpret parameters, failing commissioning.
properties.attack_vector: Malicious DCL entry contains improperly formatted URL query (e.g., using ';' delimiter). Commissioner constructs broken URL. Manufacturer server error reveals internal paths or configurations in error message.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_MTcu_Placeholder_Format
properties.claim: If key MTcu is present in CommissioningCustomFlowUrl, SHALL have value '_' (MTcu=_) as callback URL placeholder
properties.violation: Invalid MTcu placeholder format breaks callback mechanism. Commissioner cannot provide callback URL, losing return-path from manufacturer flow, requiring manual re-entry.
properties.attack_vector: Malformed MTcu in DCL causes commissioner to skip callback expansion. User completes manufacturer flow but cannot return to original commissioner, must restart commissioning with new payload entry.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_MTop_Placeholder_Format
properties.claim: If key MTop is present in CommissioningCustomFlowUrl, SHALL have value '_' (MTop=_) as onboarding payload placeholder
properties.violation: Invalid MTop placeholder prevents payload embedding. Manufacturer flow cannot receive device-specific information, breaking automated device provisioning.
properties.attack_vector: Malformed MTop causes commissioner to skip payload embedding. Manufacturer server cannot identify target device, requiring manual entry. Attacker exploits manual entry to trick user into entering attacker's device credentials instead.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_MT_Key_Reservation
properties.claim: Query keys whose name begins with MT (except MTcu, MTop) are reserved for future spec use. Manufacturers SHALL NOT include such keys.
properties.violation: Manufacturer using reserved MT-prefixed keys creates forward compatibility breaks. Future spec versions using same keys cause conflicts, breaking commissioning with updated commissioners.
properties.attack_vector: Manufacturer uses MTauth=token for custom authentication. Future spec version defines MTauth for standardized authentication. Commissioner interprets manufacturer token as spec-compliant auth, bypassing manufacturer's security checks.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_MTop_Passcode_Protection
properties.claim: When CommissioningCustomFlowUrl includes MTop key, Passcode in Onboarding Payload SHALL NOT be one usable for secure channel establishment
properties.violation: Sending real passcode to manufacturer server via URL transfers shared secret without user consent, exposing proof-of-possession credential to third-party server and network logs.
properties.attack_vector: Attacker intercepts HTTPS connection to manufacturer server (via compromised CA or server breach) and extracts passcode from MTop parameter in URL. Attacker uses passcode to commission device before or instead of legitimate user.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_MTop_Passcode_Hint
properties.claim: When MTop key present, Passcode MAY be set to 0 (invalid) to hint commissioner the passcode is not usable for secure channel establishment
properties.violation: If passcode is invalid but not set to 0, commissioner may waste time attempting commissioning with invalid credentials before detecting custom flow requirement.
properties.attack_vector: Device uses invalid passcode that's not 0. Commissioner attempts commissioning repeatedly, triggering rate limiting or account lockout. Attacker induces DoS via commissioning exhaustion.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_URL_Element_Preservation
properties.claim: Non-specified query elements and fragment field SHALL remain as obtained from DCL, including order of key/value pairs
properties.violation: Reordering or modifying non-spec query parameters breaks manufacturer's server-side parsing or security mechanisms relying on parameter order or specific values.
properties.attack_vector: Manufacturer server validates request signature based on query parameter order. Commissioner reorders parameters during expansion. Signature validation fails, commissioning breaks, user directed to error page that could be spoofed.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_URL_Expansion_Algorithm_MTcu
properties.claim: If MTcu key present, commissioner SHALL compute desired CallbackUrl and substitute '_' placeholder with URL-encoded callback URL
properties.violation: Failed or incorrect MTcu substitution breaks return path from manufacturer flow, forcing user to manually re-enter commissioning information.
properties.attack_vector: Commissioner incorrectly substitutes MTcu without URL encoding, creating malformed nested URL. Manufacturer server misparses, logs error containing internal state, or redirects to error page under attacker control.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_URL_Expansion_Algorithm_MTop
properties.claim: If MTop key present, commissioner SHALL substitute '_' placeholder with URL-encoded Manual Pairing Code or QR code body including MT: prefix
properties.violation: Failed payload embedding prevents manufacturer server from identifying device, breaking automated provisioning and forcing manual device identification.
properties.attack_vector: Commissioner fails to include MT: prefix in MTop substitution. Manufacturer server rejects request. User receives generic error, contacts support. Attacker impersonating support extracts commissioning credentials via social engineering.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_MTop_NonAppend_Constraint
properties.claim: Commissioner SHALL NOT append MTop= query key/value unless it was already present as MTop=_ in CommissioningCustomFlowUrl
properties.violation: Adding MTop when not originally present exposes which products embed payloads in URLs by DCL inspection. Attacker can target specific products knowing they leak credentials in URLs.
properties.attack_vector: Commissioner adds MTop to all Custom Flow URLs. Attacker identifies products with MTop in DCL, knows these expose passcodes server-side. Attacker focuses attacks on manufacturer servers for these products to harvest passcodes.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_CallbackUrl_Format_Ampersand
properties.claim: CallbackUrl query component SHALL use '&' delimiter between key/value pairs with 'name=<value>' format and URL encoding
properties.violation: Malformed CallbackUrl breaks manufacturer flow's return mechanism. User cannot automatically return to commissioner after custom setup.
properties.attack_vector: Commissioner generates malformed CallbackUrl. Manufacturer flow fails to parse. Error handling redirects to manufacturer support page. Attacker compromises support page to harvest user information.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_MTrop_Placeholder_Format
properties.claim: If key MTrop is present in CallbackUrl, SHALL have value '_' (MTrop=_) as returned onboarding payload placeholder
properties.violation: Invalid MTrop placeholder breaks return payload mechanism. User must manually re-enter commissioning credentials at commissioner instead of automatic resumption.
properties.attack_vector: Malformed MTrop prevents automatic payload return. User must manually transcribe new payload. Attacker performs shoulder-surfing or clipboard monitoring to steal returned payload during manual entry.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_CallbackUrl_MT_Reservation
properties.claim: Query keys in CallbackUrl whose name begins with MT (except MTrop) are reserved for future spec use
properties.violation: Commissioner using reserved MT-prefixed keys in CallbackUrl creates forward compatibility issues with future spec versions.
properties.attack_vector: Commissioner uses MTsession=token. Future spec defines MTsession standardized meaning. Manufacturer flow interprets commissioner's MTsession incorrectly, breaking return flow or leaking session data.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_CallbackUrl_Element_Preservation
properties.claim: Non-specified query elements and fragment in CallbackUrl SHALL remain as provided by commissioner, including parameter order
properties.violation: Modifying commissioner-provided CallbackUrl elements breaks commissioner's return-path security mechanisms or session tracking.
properties.attack_vector: Commissioner includes signed token in CallbackUrl. Manufacturer flow reorders parameters. Signature validation breaks. Commissioner rejects return, user stuck between commissioner and manufacturer flow.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_CallbackUrl_Expansion_MTrop
properties.claim: If MTrop present and manufacturer flow supports commissioning window opening, SHALL substitute '_' with URL-encoded QR format payload including MT: prefix
properties.violation: Failed MTrop substitution forces manual payload re-entry by user. Commissioner cannot automatically resume, increasing user friction and error opportunities.
properties.attack_vector: Manufacturer flow fails to expand MTrop. Commissioner receives callback without payload. User must scan new QR or enter code. Attacker swaps QR code with attacker's device payload during manual entry window.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_MTrop_Device_State_Consistency
properties.claim: Returned payload in MTrop SHALL match device state at moment ExpandedCallbackUrl is opened
properties.violation: Stale returned payload causes commissioning to fail when commissioner attempts to use outdated credentials. Device may have rotated keys or closed commissioning window.
properties.attack_vector: Manufacturer flow returns payload, delays callback URL opening. Device closes commissioning window. Attacker races to commission device first. Commissioner attempts commissioning with stale payload, fails, alerting user only after attacker controls device.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_MTrop_CustomFlow_Zero
properties.claim: Returned onboarding payload CustomFlow field SHALL be 0 (Standard) not original Custom Flow value
properties.violation: If returned payload still indicates Custom Flow, commissioner enters infinite redirect loop, repeatedly following custom flow URL instead of commissioning device.
properties.attack_vector: Returned payload incorrectly has CustomFlow=2. Commissioner detects Custom Flow, redirects to manufacturer URL again. User stuck in redirect loop. Attacker exploits user frustration via social engineering to gain commissioning access.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_MTrop_Alternate_Means
properties.claim: Manufacturer custom flow SHOULD provide alternate means of conveying onboarding payload (e.g., manual pairing code)
properties.violation: Without alternate payload delivery, commissioner failure to receive or parse MTrop leaves user with no path to commissioning completion.
properties.attack_vector: Commissioner has MTrop parsing bug. Manufacturer flow only provides payload via MTrop. User cannot commission. Attacker offers 'helper' tool that requires user to paste payload, harvesting commissioning credentials.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_Direct_Payload_Usability
properties.claim: If initial onboarding payload directly usable, commissioner MAY use it upon ExpandedCallbackUrl trigger or autonomously as fallback
properties.violation: Commissioner that cannot use initial payload when still valid forces unnecessary manufacturer flow, increasing attack surface and failure points.
properties.attack_vector: Initial payload is valid but commissioner ignores it, always following custom flow. Attacker compromises manufacturer URL to phish credentials. All users affected even when payload would allow direct secure commissioning.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_Commissioner_CallbackUrl_Support
properties.claim: Commissioners providing CallbackUrl SHOULD support using ExpandedCallbackUrl to trigger commissioning resumption
properties.violation: Commissioner providing CallbackUrl but not supporting callback trigger wastes bandwidth and confuses manufacturer flow about return capability.
properties.attack_vector: Commissioner provides CallbackUrl but ignores callback. Manufacturer flow returns control via callback. Nothing happens. User confused, abandons commissioning or accepts attacker's 'help' to complete setup.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_MTrop_Noexpand_On_Failure
properties.claim: If manufacturer flow failed to make device commissionable, SHALL NOT replace '_' placeholder in MTrop= key/value pair
properties.violation: Expanding MTrop when device not ready causes commissioner to attempt commissioning against non-ready device, failing repeatedly and potentially triggering security lockouts.
properties.attack_vector: Manufacturer flow fails but expands MTrop with last-known payload. Commissioner attempts commissioning repeatedly. Device implements wrong rate limiting, permanently locks out all commissioning attempts after N failures.

properties.name: Custom_Flow_Callback_Opening_Attempt
properties.claim: Manufacturer flow having received ExpandedCommissioningCustomFlowUrl SHOULD attempt to open ExpandedCallbackUrl on completion if computed and supported
properties.violation: Not opening callback forces user to manually trigger commissioner resumption, increasing friction and error opportunities.
properties.attack_vector: Manufacturer flow completes but doesn't open callback. User must manually return to commissioner. Attacker performs clipboard injection or QR code substitution during manual return process to redirect to attacker's payload.

properties.name: ESF_TC_Initial_Commission_Bit_Requirement
properties.claim: EnhancedSetupFlowOptions bit 0 indicates commissioner required to show Terms and Conditions on initial commissioning from factory-reset state
properties.violation: Skipping required ToS presentation on initial commissioning bypasses manufacturer's legal/security requirements, potentially violating regulations or leaving user uninformed of privacy implications.
properties.attack_vector: Commissioner ignores TC requirement bit. User commissions security camera without seeing data collection ToS. Manufacturer collects video without informed consent. Regulatory violation creates liability. Attacker uses this to frame manufacturer and create distrust.

properties.name: ESF_TC_Required_NoReuse_Bit
properties.claim: Bit 1 of EnhancedSetupFlowOptions when set to 1 means commissioner SHALL NOT reuse user's responses to required TC from previous product commissioning
properties.violation: Reusing TC acceptance when disallowed bypasses manufacturer's requirement for fresh consent per device, violating legal requirements or device-specific security acknowledgments.
properties.attack_vector: User accepted TC for motion sensor. Attacker tricks user into commissioning attacker's security camera using same commissioner. Commissioner reuses TC acceptance. User unaware camera has more invasive data collection terms, violating informed consent.

properties.name: ESF_TC_NonRequired_NoReuse_Bit
properties.claim: Bit 2 of EnhancedSetupFlowOptions when set to 1 means commissioner SHALL NOT reuse user's responses to non-required TC from previous product commissioning
properties.violation: Reusing optional TC acceptance when disallowed prevents user from making device-specific consent decisions (e.g., cloud storage for bedroom camera vs outdoor camera).
properties.attack_vector: User accepted cloud storage TC for outdoor camera. Commissioner auto-accepts for bedroom camera in different location. Intimate recordings uploaded to cloud without fresh consent. Privacy violation and potential blackmail exposure.

properties.name: ESF_TC_BetweenPID_NoReuse_Bit
properties.claim: Bit 3 of EnhancedSetupFlowOptions when set to 1 means commissioner SHALL NOT reuse user's TC responses from commissioning product with different PID
properties.violation: Reusing TC across PIDs when disallowed applies wrong terms to different product types, potentially accepting inappropriate privacy/security terms.
properties.attack_vector: User accepted limited TC for simple light bulb (PID_A). Commissioner reuses for manufacturer's security camera (PID_B) despite different TC required. Camera has invasive data collection user didn't consent to.

properties.name: ESF_File_Validation_Required
properties.claim: Commissioner SHALL validate ESF file using EnhancedSetupFlowTCDigest, EnhancedSetupFlowTCFileSize, and TC File Format validation
properties.violation: Without validation, attacker can substitute malicious ESF file containing phishing links, malicious HTML/JavaScript, or fraudulent terms.
properties.attack_vector: Attacker compromises ESF file host, replaces TC file with malicious version containing harvesting JavaScript in HTML tags. Commissioner skips validation, displays malicious content. User's credentials or session stolen via XSS.

properties.name: ESF_Validation_Failure_CustomFlow_Fallback
properties.claim: When ESF file not found or validation fails, commissioner SHALL proceed using Custom Commissioning Flow
properties.violation: Proceeding without valid ESF file skips manufacturer's required terms presentation, violating legal requirements or security disclosures.
properties.attack_vector: Attacker performs MitM to block ESF file download. Commissioner continues without TC presentation. User commissions device unaware of invasive data collection. Attacker exploits lack of informed consent to claim user implicitly accepted data sharing.

properties.name: ESF_TC_Primary_Focus_Presentation
properties.claim: Commissioner SHALL present TC terms giving user primary focus, text not hidden, obscured, or omitted except allowed exceptions
properties.violation: Hiding or obscuring TC defeats informed consent requirement. User accepts terms without awareness, violating legal and ethical requirements.
properties.attack_vector: Commissioner presents TC in tiny font, hidden behind other UI, or as auto-scrolling text. User clicks accept without reading. Manufacturer includes mandatory arbitration and data sale clauses user didn't see. Legal disputes arise.

properties.name: ESF_TC_Accept_Decline_Option
properties.claim: User SHALL be given option to accept or decline each provided TC text
properties.violation: Without decline option, user cannot refuse objectionable terms, violating user autonomy and informed consent principles.
properties.attack_vector: Commissioner presents TC with only 'Accept' button. User cannot decline invasive data collection terms except by abandoning device. Manufacturer claims user acceptance despite coerced acceptance.

properties.name: ESF_TC_Required_Decline_Handling
properties.claim: If user declines any text marked as required, commissioner SHALL end commissioning and notify user device cannot be commissioned due to refusal
properties.formal: ∃ TC_Text: Required(TC_Text) ∧ Declined(user, TC_Text) → EndsCommissioning(commissioner) ∧ NotifiesUser(commissioner)
properties.violation: Proceeding after required TC declined violates manufacturer's legal requirements and device security policies.
properties.attack_vector: User declines required security disclaimer. Commissioner proceeds anyway. Device commissioned in unsupported configuration. Security incident occurs. Manufacturer liable due to missing required acknowledgment. Attacker exploits to frame manufacturer.

properties.name: ESF_TC_Caching_Rules_Enforcement
properties.claim: Commissioner SHALL enforce TC caching rules based on ESF option bits 1, 2, 3 per specification table
properties.violation: Incorrect caching allows TC reuse when prohibited, bypassing fresh consent requirements and device-specific acknowledgments.
properties.attack_vector: Commissioner bug allows TC reuse across PIDs despite bit 3 set. User accepted minimal TC for light bulb, auto-applied to security camera with invasive terms. Privacy violation occurs without fresh informed consent.

properties.name: ESF_TC_NoCache_Different_VID
properties.claim: Caching of TC responses SHALL NOT be used when VID for current product differs from product where user reviewed and acknowledged TC
properties.violation: Reusing TC across vendors applies wrong manufacturer's terms to different manufacturer's device, creating legal confusion and invalid consent.
properties.attack_vector: User accepted Manufacturer_A's privacy-focused TC. Commissioner reuses for Manufacturer_B's device with invasive data collection. User believes Manufacturer_A's terms apply. Manufacturer_B collects data user didn't consent to. Privacy violation and trust breach.

properties.name: ESF_TC_Same_TC_Definition
properties.claim: TC caching SHALL NOT be used when EnhancedSetupFlowTCDigest or EnhancedSetupFlowTCRevision changed between cached and current commissioning
properties.violation: Reusing TC responses when TC content changed applies old consent to new terms, violating fresh informed consent requirement.
properties.attack_vector: Manufacturer updates TC to add invasive data collection clause. Commissioner uses cached acceptance from old TC version. User unaware of new terms. Data collected without proper consent. Regulatory violation and liability.

properties.name: ESF_TC_CustomFlow_SetValues
properties.claim: When Custom Flow used for TC display, manufacturer SHALL set TCAcknowledgementsRequired=False, TCAcceptedVersion to accepted version, TCAcknowledgements with user responses
properties.formal: DisplayedTCViaCustomFlow(device) → TCAcknowledgementsRequired(device) = False ∧ TCAcceptedVersion(device) = AcceptedVersion ∧ TCAcknowledgements(device) = UserResponses
properties.violation: Failing to set these attributes causes commissioner to re-request TC acknowledgments after custom flow, duplicating TC presentation and confusing user.
properties.attack_vector: Custom flow displays TC but doesn't set attributes. Commissioner requests TC again. User accepts second time. Attacker exploits confusion to present fraudulent TC during second round, user accepts attacker's terms thinking it's legitimate.

properties.name: ESF_TC_Software_Update_Caution
properties.claim: Extending or adding TC via software updates SHOULD be avoided to prevent consumer frustration and device dysfunction
properties.violation: Adding required TC via update may result in user declining new terms, leaving device non-functional and creating consumer frustration and potential safety issues.
properties.attack_vector: Manufacturer pushes update adding required data collection TC. User declines. Device stops functioning. Safety-critical device (e.g., CO detector) becomes non-functional. Attacker exploits consumer outrage for reputation attack against manufacturer.

properties.name: ESF_TC_Administrator_Subscribe
properties.claim: Administrator SHOULD subscribe to TCAcknowledgementsRequired attribute to detect updated TC availability
properties.violation: Without subscription, administrator unaware of TC updates. Device may limit functionality waiting for TC acknowledgment. User confused by degraded functionality.
properties.attack_vector: Device updates TC, sets TCAcknowledgementsRequired=True, limits functionality. Administrator not subscribed, doesn't notice. User frustrated by degraded service. Attacker exploits confusion via social engineering, claiming to 'fix' issue while installing backdoor.

properties.name: ESF_TC_Administrator_Present_Updated
properties.claim: Administrator SHOULD present updated TC to user when TC updates available, in similar manner to commissioning flow presentation
properties.violation: Not presenting updated TC leaves device in limited functionality state or proceeds without required fresh consent.
properties.attack_vector: Updated TC adds invasive cloud storage terms. Administrator doesn't present. Device operates with new invasive data collection without user awareness. Privacy violation and potential regulatory liability.

properties.name: ESF_TC_SetTCAcknowledgements
properties.claim: User's updated TC responses SHALL be propagated back to node with SetTCAcknowledgements command
properties.violation: Without propagating responses, device remains unaware of user's acceptance, continuing limited functionality or violating user's declination.
properties.attack_vector: User declines updated cloud storage TC. Administrator fails to send SetTCAcknowledgements. Device continues cloud uploads despite declination. Privacy violation. Data leaked to cloud that user explicitly declined.

properties.name: ESF_TC_Limited_Functionality_Caution
properties.claim: Device MAY limit functionality until new TC acknowledgments received, but this SHOULD be avoided to prevent user frustration
properties.violation: Limiting functionality creates consumer frustration and potential safety issues if device provides critical services.
properties.attack_vector: Smart lock requires TC update, stops functioning until acknowledged. User locked out of home. Safety hazard emerges. Attacker exploits consumer outrage for reputation damage or induces user to bypass security via alternate means attacker can exploit.

properties.name: ESF_TC_TCUpdateDeadline_Notification
properties.claim: If device enforces TC update requirement, SHALL inform user in TC and use TCUpdateDeadline to indicate deadline
properties.violation: Without deadline notification, user surprised by sudden functionality loss, creating safety issues, user frustration, and lack of informed consent.
properties.attack_vector: Device updates TC, sets short deadline without notification. Deadline passes, device stops working. User in emergency cannot use device. Safety incident occurs. Attacker exploits incident for liability claim or reputation damage.

properties.name: ESF_TC_Administrator_Deadline_Notification
properties.claim: If TCUpdateDeadline non-null, administrator SHOULD inform user of deadline to avoid device functionality impact
properties.violation: User unaware of deadline, misses it, device functionality impacted. User frustrated and potentially unsafe if device critical.
properties.attack_vector: Security system requires TC update with deadline. Administrator doesn't notify user. Deadline passes, system disables. Home left unsecured. Burglar exploits timing window of disabled security.

properties.name: ESF_TC_TERMS_CHANGED_Status_Code
properties.claim: Interactions failing due to TC update requirement SHALL return TERMS_AND_CONDITIONS_CHANGED failure status code
properties.violation: Without proper status code, administrator and user cannot diagnose TC-related functionality limitation, prolonging confusion and degraded service.
properties.attack_vector: Device limits functionality due to TC update but returns generic error. User and administrator confused. Attacker impersonating support extracts device credentials or user information claiming to troubleshoot 'error'.

properties.name: ESF_TC_MaintenanceUrl_Direction
properties.claim: Administrator encountering TERMS_AND_CONDITIONS_CHANGED status SHOULD direct user to EnhancedSetupFlowMaintenanceUrl for resolution
properties.violation: Without clear direction to maintenance URL, user lacks guidance for resolving TC issue, prolonging degraded service.
properties.attack_vector: Administrator shows generic error without maintenance URL. User searches web for help. Attacker's SEO-optimized fake support page appears. User follows attacker's instructions, compromising device or credentials.

properties.name: ESF_TC_Restore_Functionality_On_Accept
properties.claim: Upon satisfactory acceptance of updated TC, device SHALL operate with full related functionality based on user responses
properties.violation: Device continues limited functionality despite TC acceptance, creating persistent degraded service and user frustration.
properties.attack_vector: Device bugs causes it to ignore TC acceptance, continuing degraded mode. User frustrated, contacts attacker-run 'support' for help, compromises device following attacker's troubleshooting instructions.

properties.name: ESF_TC_Reuse_Update_Across_Devices
properties.claim: When TC reuse allowed, administrator MAY set latest acknowledgments to all applicable devices after user accepts new TC revision
properties.violation: Not propagating TC acceptance across devices forces user to acknowledge same TC multiple times per device, increasing friction.
properties.attack_vector: User accepts updated TC for device_1. Must re-accept for 50 other devices manually. User frustrated, starts auto-accepting without reading. Attacker exploits auto-accept behavior by injecting fraudulent TC during acceptance sequence.

properties.name: ESF_File_UTF8_Encoding
properties.claim: ESF file encoding SHALL be UTF-8
properties.violation: Non-UTF-8 encoding causes misrendering of international characters, displaying corrupted TC text, preventing informed consent.
properties.attack_vector: ESF file uses non-UTF-8 encoding. Commissioner renders TC with corrupted characters. User cannot read TC properly. Attacker claims user accepted unreadable terms, using legal ambiguity to exploit user.

properties.name: ESF_SchemaVersion_Field_Required
properties.claim: ESF file SHALL include schemaVersion field indicating JSON schema format version
properties.violation: Without schema version, commissioner cannot determine how to parse file. Forward/backward compatibility breaks, causing commissioning failures.
properties.attack_vector: ESF file missing schemaVersion. New commissioner using updated schema misparses file. Displays wrong TC sections or corrupted text. User accepts thinking it's legitimate TC. Attacker exploits to claim user accepted attacker's terms.

properties.name: ESF_EsfRevision_Field_Required
properties.claim: ESF file SHALL include esfRevision field (16-bit integer) matching EnhancedSetupFlowTCRevision in DCL
properties.violation: Mismatch between file revision and DCL causes stale or wrong TC file to be used, applying outdated terms or wrong manufacturer's TC.
properties.attack_vector: Attacker compromises ESF file host, serving old revision with less restrictive terms. Commissioner doesn't verify revision match (only digest). User accepts outdated terms. Manufacturer cannot prove user accepted current terms. Legal disputes arise.

properties.name: ESF_DefaultCountry_Field_Required
properties.claim: ESF file SHALL include defaultCountry field with ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code for fallback when user's country not defined
properties.violation: Without default country, commissioner cannot display TC for users in countries not explicitly included, preventing commissioning or showing wrong jurisdiction TC.
properties.attack_vector: User in country_X not in ESF file. No default country set. Commissioner shows null TC or wrong country. User commissions device without proper legal terms. Regulatory violation in country_X jurisdiction. Attacker exploits to claim no valid consent.

properties.name: ESF_CountryEntries_Field_Required
properties.claim: ESF file SHALL include countryEntries field with list of TC entries per country, containing at least one entry
properties.violation: Without country entries, no TC text available for display. Commissioning cannot proceed with TC requirement.
properties.attack_vector: Empty countryEntries causes commissioner error. User cannot commission. Attacker offers 'bypass tool' requiring device credentials. Tool installs backdoor while claiming to fix commissioning.

properties.name: ESF_DefaultLanguage_Field_Required
properties.claim: ESF file SHALL include defaultLanguage field (ISO 639-1 code) for each country entry indicating fallback language
properties.violation: Without default language, commissioner cannot display TC for users with languages not explicitly included, preventing commissioning or showing wrong language TC.
properties.attack_vector: User speaks language_Y not in country entry. No default language. Commissioner shows null or corrupted TC. User cannot read, accepts blindly or abandons commissioning. Attacker exploits language gap for confusion-based attacks.

properties.name: ESF_LanguageEntries_Field_Required
properties.claim: ESF file SHALL include languageEntries field for each country entry with TC texts per language, containing at least one entry
properties.violation: Without language entries, no TC text available for user's language. User cannot read TC, preventing informed consent.
properties.attack_vector: Empty languageEntries prevents TC display. Commissioner error. User frustrated, accepts attacker's fake 'translated TC' document that actually contains attacker's data harvesting consent.

properties.name: ESF_Ordinal_Field_Required
properties.claim: ESF file SHALL include ordinal field for each TC text specifying which bit number in TCAcknowledgements bitmap this text maps to
properties.violation: Without ordinal mapping, commissioner cannot correlate user responses to TC sections. Device cannot determine which terms user accepted.
properties.attack_vector: Missing ordinal causes commissioner to mismap user responses. User declines invasive data collection (bit 3) but commissioner sets bit 5. Device interprets as accepting data collection. Privacy violation without consent.

properties.name: ESF_Ordinal_Stability_Required
properties.claim: Each bit in TCAcknowledgements bitmap SHALL keep same purpose through product life to ensure correct interpretation across firmware versions
properties.formal: ∀ bit ∈ TCAcknowledgements: Purpose(bit, version_N) = Purpose(bit, version_N+1)
properties.violation: Changing bit meanings across versions causes misinterpretation of user consent. Old acceptance incorrectly applied to new features or vice versa.
properties.attack_vector: Firmware v1: bit 2 = basic telemetry. User accepts. Firmware v2: bit 2 = video upload. Device interprets old acceptance as video consent. Sensitive video uploaded without consent. Privacy violation and potential blackmail exposure.

properties.name: ESF_Required_Field_Required
properties.claim: ESF file SHALL include required field (boolean) for each TC text indicating if user must accept for device functionality
properties.violation: Without required flag, commissioner cannot determine if user's declination should block commissioning, allowing commissioning without required consent.
properties.attack_vector: Missing required flag causes commissioner to treat all TC as optional. User declines mandatory safety disclaimer. Device commissioned anyway. Safety incident occurs. Manufacturer cannot prove user was warned, faces liability.

properties.name: ESF_Title_Field_Required
properties.claim: ESF file SHALL include title field (string) for each TC text representing title for TC subsection, may contain limited HTML tags for formatting
properties.violation: Missing title reduces clarity of TC presentation. Malicious HTML tags enable XSS attacks or misleading formatting.
properties.attack_vector: Title contains <script> tag. Commissioner doesn't sanitize. XSS executes in commissioner context. Attacker steals commissioner session tokens or injects fake TC acceptance, claiming user consented to attacker's terms.

properties.name: ESF_Text_Field_Required
properties.claim: ESF file SHALL include text field (string) for each TC text containing terms to present, may use limited HTML tags only
properties.violation: Missing text provides no TC for user to review. Malicious HTML enables XSS, phishing links, or visual deception.
properties.attack_vector: TC text contains <iframe> loading attacker.com/phish. Commissioner renders iframe. User enters credentials thinking it's manufacturer auth. Attacker harvests credentials. User and device compromised.

properties.name: ESF_HTML_Tag_Restriction
properties.claim: TC title and text fields SHALL only use specified subset of HTML tags; other HTML tags SHALL NOT be used
properties.violation: Unrestricted HTML allows XSS, phishing, malicious scripts, or misleading rendering compromising commissioner and user security.
properties.attack_vector: TC includes <script>sendToAttacker(localStorage)</script>. Commissioner renders without sanitization. Script exfiltrates commissioner's stored credentials, session tokens, and device keys to attacker. Complete compromise.

properties.name: ESF_Locale_Selection_BCP47
properties.claim: Locale selected for TC texts SHALL be BCP47 language tag with user's preferred language and ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code representing device location
properties.violation: Wrong locale causes mismatched legal jurisdiction TC or wrong language, preventing informed consent.
properties.attack_vector: Device in country_A shows country_B TC with different legal requirements. User accepts thinking country_A laws apply. Manufacturer exploits country_B's weaker privacy laws to collect data prohibited in country_A. Privacy violation.

properties.name: ESF_Locale_Fallback_To_Default
properties.claim: If requested locale not found, default language/country SHALL be used in its place
properties.violation: Without fallback, user without explicit locale support cannot see TC, blocking commissioning.
properties.attack_vector: User's locale not found, no fallback. Commissioner error. User searches for help. Attacker's fake support site offers 'commissioning bypass' requiring device admin credentials, compromising device.

properties.name: Fallback_URL_Population_Required
properties.claim: Devices anticipating commissioning failure (e.g., non-mandatory commissioning tech) SHALL populate CommissioningFallbackUrl and DiscoveryCapabilitiesBitmask in DCL
properties.violation: Without fallback URL, commissioner cannot guide user when commissioning predictably fails, leaving device unrecoverable.
properties.attack_vector: Device only supports WiFi commissioning (not mandatory). Commissioner without WiFi attempts commissioning, fails. No fallback URL. User searches web for help. Attacker's SEO-optimized fake support captures user, directs to malicious app.

properties.name: Fallback_Manual_Code_VID_PID_Required
properties.claim: For Manual Pairing Code for products requiring fallback mechanism, Encoding Method with Vendor and Product IDs SHALL be used for DCL lookup
properties.violation: Without VID/PID in manual code, commissioner cannot perform DCL lookup to retrieve fallback URL, leaving user stranded.
properties.attack_vector: Manual code lacks VID/PID. Commissioning fails. Commissioner cannot identify device for DCL lookup. User cannot reach fallback URL. Attacker offers 'device identifier tool' requiring admin credentials, compromising device.

properties.name: Fallback_URL_Optional_Population
properties.claim: For products not anticipating systematic failure, CommissioningFallbackUrl MAY be populated to provide manufacturer guidance for any failure reason
properties.violation: Omitting fallback URL for unanticipated failures leaves user without guidance, increasing support costs and user frustration.
properties.attack_vector: Unexpected commissioning failure occurs. No fallback URL. User searches web. Attacker's fake support page ranks high in search. User follows attacker's 'fix', installing backdoored firmware update.

properties.name: Fallback_Commissioner_Systematic_Failure_Usage
properties.claim: When commissioner encounters systematic failure (capability mismatch) with no alternative guidance means, SHALL use CommissioningFallbackUrl to guide user
properties.violation: Ignoring fallback URL leaves user without resolution path, abandoning device or seeking potentially malicious third-party help.
properties.attack_vector: Commissioner detects capability mismatch but ignores fallback URL. User stuck. User searches for help. Attacker's forum post suggests 'flash custom firmware' at attacker URL. User installs backdoored firmware.

properties.name: Fallback_Impossible_URL_User_Notification
properties.claim: When using fallback URL technically impossible (headless, no internet) or offline commissioning, commissioner SHOULD inform user of assumed failure reason
properties.violation: Without informing user of failure reason, user lacks actionable information for resolution, prolonging commissioning failure.
properties.attack_vector: Headless commissioner fails commissioning, doesn't inform why. User assumes device broken, returns it. Attacker purchases returned device at discount, successfully commissions using proper commissioner, gets cheap device.

properties.name: Fallback_Transient_Failure_Retry_Or_Fallback
properties.claim: When commissioner encounters transient failure, MAY retry; also MAY use CommissioningFallbackUrl to guide user
properties.violation: Not retrying or providing fallback for transient failures increases commissioning failure rate unnecessarily.
properties.attack_vector: Transient network glitch causes commissioning failure. Commissioner doesn't retry or offer fallback. User perceives device as broken. Returns device or seeks third-party help, exposing to attacker-controlled 'support' services.

properties.name: Fallback_URL_Construction_Same_As_CustomFlow
properties.claim: CommissioningFallbackUrl MAY be constructed same way as CommissioningCustomFlowUrl including VID/PID-specific URLs and query components (MTop, MTcu, MTrop)
properties.violation: Inconsistent URL format between custom flow and fallback increases complexity and bug likelihood in commissioner implementations.
properties.attack_vector: Commissioner implements custom flow URL expansion but not fallback URL expansion. Fallback URL uses MTop/MTcu. Commissioner passes unexpanded URL. Manufacturer server error leaks internal config in error message used by attacker for reconnaissance.

properties.name: Standard_Flow_DCL_Mandatory_Usage
properties.claim: Matter device manufacturers SHALL use Distributed Compliance Ledger to provide commissioners with information for initial and secondary commissioning
properties.violation: Not using DCL prevents commissioners from accessing standardized device information, breaking interoperability and forcing bilateral arrangements.
properties.attack_vector: Manufacturer omits DCL entries. Commissioner cannot retrieve commissioning instructions or fallback URLs. User must contact manufacturer directly. Attacker impersonates manufacturer support, extracts device admin credentials or directs user to malicious app.

properties.name: DCL_Optimal_Flow_Fields_Recommended
properties.claim: Manufacturers SHOULD use DCL to provide user guide links, manufacturer app links, and pre-setup info for optimal commissioning without bilateral arrangements
properties.formal: ShouldPopulate(DCL, UserGuideLink) ∧ ShouldPopulate(DCL, MfgAppLink) ∧ ShouldPopulate(DCL, PreSetupInfo)
properties.violation: Omitting recommended DCL fields reduces commissioning UX quality, forcing users to search for information externally with phishing risks.
properties.attack_vector: DCL missing user guide link. User searches for device manual online. Attacker's SEO-optimized fake manual site provides 'updated firmware' download link. User installs backdoored firmware thinking it's legitimate update.

properties.name: Commissioner_Commissioning_Attempt_Transparency
properties.claim: When commissioning fails, commissioner MAY reference DCL fields (FallbackUrl, UserManualUrl, SupportUrl, ProductUrl) to assist user in resolution
properties.formal: CommissioningFails → MayReference(commissioner, DCL.FallbackUrl ∪ DCL.UserManualUrl ∪ DCL.SupportUrl ∪ DCL.ProductUrl)
properties.violation: Not providing DCL-based assistance leaves user without official resolution paths, increasing likelihood of seeking malicious third-party help.
properties.attack_vector: Commissioning fails. Commissioner provides no DCL-based help links. User googles device name. Attacker's fake support site ranks high. User follows attacker's instructions, exposing device credentials.

properties.name: Commissioner_DCL_Guidance_Reference
properties.claim: Commissioner SHOULD reference DCL fields like CommissioningModeInitialStepsHint and CommissioningModeInitialStepsInstruction to guide user during commissioning
properties.violation: Ignoring DCL guidance fields prevents commissioner from providing manufacturer-specific instructions, causing commissioning failures.
properties.attack_vector: Device requires specific button sequence to enter commissioning mode. DCL has instructions. Commissioner ignores them, provides generic guidance. User cannot commission. Seeks help, encounters attacker pretending to offer missing instructions in exchange for account credentials.

properties.name: Standard_Flow_Advertisement_Period_Resumption
properties.claim: When Standard Flow device stops advertising after advertisement period, manufacturer SHOULD provide guidance to bring device back into advertising mode via CommissioningModeInitialStepsHint
properties.violation: Without resumption guidance, user cannot re-enter commissioning mode after advertisement period, device becomes unrecoverable without manufacturer documentation.
properties.attack_vector: Advertisement window expires. User attempts commissioning too late. No guidance to restart advertising. Device appears broken. User attempts factory reset using wrong procedure, permanently bricking device.

properties.name: Implicit_State_Transition_FactoryNew_To_Advertising
properties.claim: Standard Flow device SHALL transition from FactoryNew state to Advertising state automatically on power-on without user interaction
properties.formal: State = FactoryNew ∧ FlowType = Standard ∧ Event = PowerOn → State' = Advertising ∧ ¬RequiresUserInteraction
properties.violation: Requiring user interaction for Standard Flow contradicts Standard Flow definition, causing commissioning confusion.
properties.attack_vector: Device claims Standard Flow but requires button press (User-Intent behavior). Commissioner expects auto-advertising, doesn't instruct user to press button. Commissioning never starts. User frustrated, returns device or accepts attacker's 'helper app'.

properties.name: Implicit_State_Transition_UserIntent_PowerOn_No_Advertising
properties.claim: User-Intent Flow device SHALL remain in FactoryNew state on power-on, requiring explicit user intent signal to transition to Advertising state
properties.formal: State = FactoryNew ∧ FlowType = UserIntent ∧ Event = PowerOn → State' = FactoryNew; State = FactoryNew ∧ Event = UserIntentSignal → State' = Advertising
properties.violation: Auto-advertising on power-on violates User-Intent definition, enabling drive-by commissioning attacks.
properties.attack_vector: User-Intent device auto-advertises on power. Attacker positioned near device during unboxing. Attacker commissions device before user provides intent signal. User unaware device already commissioned by attacker.

properties.name: Implicit_State_Transition_Custom_Requires_Manufacturer_Steps
properties.claim: Custom Flow device SHALL require manufacturer-guided steps before transitioning to Commissionable state accessible by any commissioner
properties.formal: State = FactoryNew ∧ FlowType = Custom → RequiresManufacturerSteps before State' = Commissionable
properties.violation: Allowing commissioning without manufacturer steps violates Custom Flow definition, bypassing intended security setup.
properties.attack_vector: Device claims Custom Flow but allows direct commissioning. Attacker skips manufacturer's account binding and ToS acceptance. Device commissioned without audit trail. Security incident occurs, manufacturer cannot identify responsible party.

properties.name: Implicit_Proof_Of_Possession_Pairing_Code
properties.claim: Proof-of-possession SHALL be established via valid pairing code (QR or Manual) requiring physical device access
properties.violation: Without proper proof-of-possession, remote attacker can commission device without physical access.
properties.attack_vector: Device uses default or easily guessable pairing code. Attacker performs network scan, finds commissionable devices, brute-forces or uses default codes, commissions devices remotely en masse.

properties.name: Implicit_DCL_Integrity_Trusted_Source
properties.claim: Commissioner SHALL retrieve DCL information from trusted, authenticated source with integrity protection
properties.violation: Untrusted or unvalidated DCL allows attacker to poison commissioning metadata, redirecting to phishing sites or wrong configurations.
properties.attack_vector: Commissioner retrieves DCL over unencrypted connection. Attacker performs MitM, modifies CommissioningCustomFlowUrl to attacker.com/phish. User redirected to phishing site, credentials harvested.

properties.name: Implicit_URL_HTTPS_Required
properties.claim: All commissioning-related URLs (Custom Flow, Fallback, ESF TC URL, Maintenance) SHALL use HTTPS scheme with valid certificate validation
properties.violation: HTTP URLs allow MitM attacks, enabling credential harvesting, phishing, and malicious content injection.
properties.attack_vector: CommissioningCustomFlowUrl uses HTTP. Attacker performs MitM, serves fake manufacturer page harvesting user's account credentials and device pairing code. Both user account and device compromised.

properties.name: Implicit_ESF_File_Host_Security
properties.claim: ESF TC file SHALL be hosted on secure server with HTTPS, validated certificates, and integrity protection via digest verification
properties.violation: Insecure hosting allows attacker to substitute malicious ESF file with phishing content or malicious HTML/JavaScript.
properties.attack_vector: ESF file on HTTP server. Attacker performs MitM, replaces TC file with version containing <script> tag exfiltrating commissioner's stored credentials. Commissioner renders malicious script, credentials stolen.

properties.name: Implicit_Commissioner_URL_Validation
properties.claim: Commissioner SHALL validate URLs from DCL before using (HTTPS scheme, valid certificate, no malicious patterns)
properties.violation: Using unvalidated URLs exposes user to phishing, malware, and protocol downgrade attacks.
properties.attack_vector: Compromised DCL entry contains javascript: URL. Commissioner follows without validation. Malicious script executes in commissioner context, exfiltrating commissioning credentials and device keys.

properties.name: Implicit_Commissioning_Window_Time_Limit
properties.claim: Device advertising SHALL have time limit (e.g., 15 minutes) after which device stops advertising until re-triggered
properties.formal: StartAdvertising(device) → StopsAfterTimeout(device, timeout) where timeout ≤ MaxAllowedPeriod
properties.violation: Unlimited advertising window extends attack surface indefinitely, allowing attacker unlimited time for commissioning attempts.
properties.attack_vector: Device advertises indefinitely. Attacker performs slow brute-force attack over weeks against pairing code. Eventually succeeds, commissioning device long after user setup period ended and owner left commissioning network behind.

properties.name: Implicit_CallbackUrl_Security
properties.claim: CallbackUrl provided by commissioner SHALL include authentication/session tokens to prevent callback interception attacks
properties.violation: Unprotected callback URL allows attacker to open callback with malicious payload, injecting attacker's device credentials into user's commissioning flow.
properties.attack_vector: Commissioner provides CallbackUrl without auth token. Attacker observes URL pattern, constructs fake callback with attacker's device payload. Opens callback. Commissioner accepts attacker's payload, attempts to commission attacker's device instead of user's.

properties.name: Implicit_MTrop_Freshness
properties.claim: Returned payload in MTrop SHALL be freshly generated at callback time, not reused from earlier generation
properties.violation: Stale payload allows attacker to replay old callback with outdated credentials that may still be partially valid.
properties.attack_vector: Manufacturer flow generates MTrop early, waits hours before callback. Device rotates commissioning credentials. Attacker intercepts old callback, replays later. Commissioner attempts commissioning with stale creds, triggering race condition that partially succeeds, leaving device in inconsistent state exploitable by attacker.

properties.name: Implicit_TC_Cache_Storage_Security
properties.claim: Commissioner caching TC responses SHALL store cached data securely with encryption and integrity protection
properties.violation: Unprotected TC cache allows attacker to read user's acceptance history or modify cache to inject fraudulent acceptances.
properties.attack_vector: Commissioner stores TC cache in plaintext local file. Attacker gains file system access, modifies cache to indicate user accepted invasive data collection for all manufacturer devices. Commissioner reuses fraudulent acceptance, user's privacy violated without consent.

properties.name: Implicit_TC_UpdateRequired_Timing_Attack_Prevention
properties.claim: Device SHALL set TCAcknowledgementsRequired atomically with TCMinRequiredVersion to prevent race conditions
properties.violation: Non-atomic update creates race window where administrator observes new required version but TCAcknowledgementsRequired still false, missing update notification.
properties.attack_vector: Device updates TCMinRequiredVersion first. Administrator reads it, sees same version as cached. Then device sets TCAcknowledgementsRequired=true. Administrator missed notification. TC update never presented. Device limits functionality, user confused.

properties.name: Implicit_DCL_VID_PID_Binding
properties.claim: DCL entries SHALL be cryptographically bound to VID/PID to prevent DCL poisoning attacks
properties.formal: DCLEntry(VID, PID) → CryptographicallyBound(DCLEntry, VID, PID) ∧ VerifiableByCommissioner(Binding)
properties.violation: Unbound DCL entries allow attacker to inject false entries for arbitrary VID/PID combinations, redirecting commissioning to malicious sites.
properties.attack_vector: Attacker creates fake DCL entry for popular device's VID/PID with CommissioningCustomFlowUrl pointing to attacker.com/phish. Commissioner retrieves entry, redirects all users of that device to phishing site. Mass credential harvest.

properties.name: Implicit_Commissioning_Network_Isolation
properties.claim: Commissioning SHALL occur on isolated network where only intended commissioner and device communicate during sensitive phases
properties.violation: Commissioning on shared network allows attacker to observe, intercept, or race against legitimate commissioner.
properties.attack_vector: User commissions device on public WiFi. Attacker on same network observes commissioning traffic, identifies device. Attacker quickly sends malicious configuration before legitimate commissioner completes. Device accepts attacker's config in race condition.

assumptions.statement: Commissioner can reliably detect whether device is in factory-new state versus already commissioned
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_002, PROP_009 fail - attacker could force device to masquerade as factory-new when already commissioned by attacker, enabling hidden takeover

assumptions.statement: Physical access to device is required to view QR code or Manual Pairing Code on device or packaging
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_004, PROP_012, PROP_018, PROP_104 fail - proof-of-possession breaks down, enabling remote commissioning attacks

assumptions.statement: DCL infrastructure is trustworthy, authenticated, and integrity-protected
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_005, PROP_013, PROP_021, PROP_022, PROP_096, PROP_105, PROP_114 fail - entire commissioning flow can be redirected to attacker-controlled infrastructure

assumptions.statement: Commissioner validates HTTPS certificates properly and prevents downgrade attacks
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_022, PROP_106, PROP_107, PROP_108 fail - MitM attacks possible on all URL-based flows

assumptions.statement: User can distinguish legitimate manufacturer websites/apps from phishing sites
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_023, PROP_025, PROP_092 fail - users following URLs vulnerable to phishing regardless of secure transport

assumptions.statement: Commissioner has access to secure storage for caching TC responses with encryption and integrity protection
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_059, PROP_060, PROP_061, PROP_112 fail - cached TC responses can be read or tampered with by attacker

assumptions.statement: Device clocks are sufficiently accurate for time-based constraints like TCUpdateDeadline
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_068, PROP_069 fail - deadline enforcement unreliable, devices may prematurely disable functionality or ignore expired deadlines

assumptions.statement: User has internet connectivity during commissioning for Custom Flow and ESF TC retrieval
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_026, PROP_027 fail - custom flow devices become uncommissionable in offline environments

assumptions.statement: Manufacturer servers hosting Custom Flow URLs and ESF TC files are secure and not compromised
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_022, PROP_054, PROP_107 fail - compromised servers can serve phishing content, malicious TC files, or harvest credentials

assumptions.statement: User interaction signals for User-Intent Flow are tamper-resistant and cannot be remotely triggered
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_010 fails - attacker could remotely trigger user intent signal, enabling commissioning without physical presence

assumptions.statement: Pairing codes are generated with sufficient entropy to prevent brute-force attacks within advertising window
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_104, PROP_109 fail - attacker can brute-force pairing code during advertising window

assumptions.statement: Commissioners properly sanitize and sandbox HTML content in TC texts before rendering
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_084, PROP_085, PROP_086 fail - XSS attacks possible even with restricted HTML tag set

assumptions.statement: Network transport during commissioning is secured against eavesdropping after initial pairing
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_104, PROP_115 fail - attacker can intercept commissioning credentials and device configuration

assumptions.statement: Device firmware cannot be downgraded to versions with weaker commissioning security
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_082 fails - attacker downgrades firmware, exploits old TC bit mappings to inject false acceptances

assumptions.statement: Administrator has authenticated, secure channel to device for setting TC acknowledgments
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_066, PROP_072 fail - attacker can inject fake TC acceptances or block legitimate ones

assumptions.statement: CommissioningCustomFlowUrl and CommissioningFallbackUrl length limits prevent buffer overflow or URL parsing attacks
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_028, PROP_034, PROP_095 fail - oversized URLs cause parsing errors, memory corruption, or DoS in commissioner

assumptions.statement: Manufacturer-provided apps for Custom Flow are vetted and cannot contain malware
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_027 fails - custom flow requires malicious app, compromising user devices and commissioned Matter devices

assumptions.statement: ESF file hosting has CDN or availability guarantees to prevent commissioning DoS
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_055 fails gracefully but PROP_050 enforcement broken - DoS on ESF file hosting blocks all commissioning requiring TC

assumptions.statement: CallbackUrl token generation is cryptographically secure and prevents guessing
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_110 fails - attacker guesses callback tokens, injects malicious payloads into users' commissioning sessions

assumptions.statement: Commissioner can detect and prevent infinite redirect loops in Custom Flow URL following
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_044 fails - malicious or buggy returned payloads cause commissioner to loop infinitely, DoS commissioning

assumptions.statement: User has single active commissioning session; multiple concurrent commissioners are prevented or tracked
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_115 fails - multiple commissioners racing creates confusion about which one user authorized, enabling attacker commissioner to blend in

assumptions.statement: Device manufacturer maintains DCL entries with current, accurate information throughout product lifecycle
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_096, PROP_097, PROP_098, PROP_099, PROP_100 fail - stale or incorrect DCL information breaks commissioning or misguides users

assumptions.statement: Advertising window timeout is enforced by device, not relying solely on network availability
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_109 fails - attacker maintains persistent network isolation, device advertises indefinitely, extended brute-force window

assumptions.statement: Returned payload in MTrop is cryptographically bound to specific commissioning session to prevent cross-session replay
assumptions.impact_if_violated: PROP_111 fails - attacker captures MTrop from one session, replays in another user's session, injecting attacker's device

fsmReferenceProperties.critical_property: Automatic transition to Advertising on PowerOn without user interaction required
No automatic transition on PowerOn; requires explicit UserIntentSignal to enter Advertising
Requires ManufacturerGuidedSteps completion before Commissionable state accessible
Time-limited state; transitions to Idle after timeout unless commissioning completes
Requires valid proof-of-possession via pairing code for secure channel establishment
TC updates can trigger TCAcknowledgementsRequired, potentially limiting functionality until acknowledgment
Device may limit functionality; interactions return TERMS_AND_CONDITIONS_CHANGED until resolved
Commissioner redirects to expanded CommissioningCustomFlowUrl, awaiting return via ExpandedCallbackUrl
ESF file validated, TC presented to user; required TC declination terminates commissioning
Commissioner uses CommissioningFallbackUrl to guide user to manufacturer resolution path

additionalInsights:
Several critical emergent risks and design weaknesses identified:

1. **Custom Flow Single Point of Failure**: Custom Commissioning Flow creates hard dependency on manufacturer infrastructure availability and security. If manufacturer's servers are compromised, DDoS'd, or shut down (company bankruptcy), all Custom Flow devices become uncom missionable. ESF provides partial mitigation but only if device implements it.

2. **DCL as Critical Trust Anchor**: The entire commissioning security model depends on DCL integrity. DCL poisoning attack (if binding insufficient per PROP_114) is catastrophic - attacker can redirect all commissioning attempts for arbitrary VID/PID to phishing infrastructure. Spec should require DCL signatures and validation mechanism.

3. **TC Caching Cross-Device Attack Surface**: TC caching rules are complex (8 combinations in table). Implementation bugs likely. Incorrect caching allows privacy violations via acceptance reuse when prohibited. Worse, cleared TC cache on one device could affect administrator's behavior for other devices, creating dependencies.

4. **HTML in TC is XSS Risk**: Even restricted HTML tag set allows some attack vectors: <h1 onclick='...''> if onclick not stripped, deeply nested tags causing DoS, or <a href='javascript:...'> if href validation missing. Spec should require explicit rendering in sandboxed context.

5. **Returned Payload (MTrop) Race Conditions**: Time gap between manufacturer flow generating MTrop and callback being opened creates multiple race windows: device commissioning window closes, device is commissioned by attacker first, device rotates keys, or replay attacks. Freshness binding insufficient without session binding (ASSUM_024).

6. **User-Intent Flow Definition Ambiguity**: Spec says 'beyond application of power' but doesn't specify security properties of intent signal. Is button press sufficient? Could be remotely triggered via RF, sound, light, etc. Needs explicit tamper-resistance requirement.

7. **Advertisement Window as Attack Surface**: 15-minute advertising window provides significant brute-force opportunity if pairing code entropy insufficient. No rate limiting mentioned. Fast network attacker could attempt thousands of codes in window.

8. **URL Expansion Side Channels**: MTop parameter in URL leaks device identity and partial pairing info to manufacturer server and any intermediaries (proxies, CDNs). Even with HTTPS, TLS session resumption and traffic analysis may allow tracking. Privacy risk not addressed.

9. **Offline Commissioning Gap**: Spec acknowledges Custom Flow requires internet (PROP_026) but no fallback for offline environments (factories, secure facilities, boats, remote locations). Creates commissioning impossible situations.

10. **TC Update Force-Function Conflict**: Allowing devices to limit functionality until TC acceptance (PROP_067) conflicts with user control and safety. Smart lock could lock user out. Medical device could stop functioning. Spec says SHOULD AVOID but still allows. Regulatory risk.

11. **Callback URL as Open Redirect**: Commissioner-provided CallbackUrl could be exploited as open redirect if commissioner doesn't validate return destination. Attacker tricks user into loading manufactured callback URL, commissioner redirects to attacker site maintaining commissioner context.

12. **Flow Type Consistency Triangle**: Three consistency properties (PROP_003, PROP_020; PROP_005, PROP_013, PROP_021) create triangle: OnboardingPayload.CustomFlowBits, Device.ActualFlowType, DCL.CommissioningCustomFlow. Any mismatch breaks commissioning unpredictably. No priority resolution specified.
